description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a distinctive ribbed appearance when viewed from above. The stone appears to be a dark, possibly granite or marble material, with a slightly mottled texture visible between the grooves. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The block is solid and heavy, likely used as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments such as sidewalks, plazas, or parks. Its robust construction and hard surface suggest it is designed to withstand significant force and environmental exposure.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, giving it a ribbed, striped appearance. The sides are smooth and the material appears dense and heavy.
description_view_0: Front view showing the block's rectangular face and the horizontal grooves running across the top surface.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the parallel grooves, with the grooves running perpendicular to the viewer.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same horizontal grooves and smooth vertical face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep, parallel grooves and their regular spacing.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
